allowed_vulnerability_classes.name: Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR)
allowed_vulnerability_classes.description: Attacker accesses or modifies resources belonging to another user by manipulating object identifiers in API requests.
in_scope_surfaces: GET /api/offers/:id — buyer can read another user's offer details
POST /api/offers/:id/accept — buyer can accept an offer on a listing they don't own
PATCH /api/listings/:id — seller_id check can be bypassed

allowed_vulnerability_classes.name: External Control of Assumed-Immutable Data
allowed_vulnerability_classes.description: Attacker tampers with client-supplied values that the server treats as trusted, such as price or status fields.
in_scope_surfaces: POST /api/listings — price field accepts attacker-supplied values without server-side floor check
POST /api/offers/:id/accept — final_price derivation from unvalidated offer state

allowed_vulnerability_classes.name: Improper Access Control
allowed_vulnerability_classes.description: Authenticated but unauthorised user can perform actions reserved for other roles or resource owners.
in_scope_surfaces: DELETE /api/listings/:id — missing or bypassable ownership check
GET /api/offers/listing/:listingId — buyer can enumerate all offers on any listing
GET /api/users/me/dashboard — user can access another user's dashboard metrics via parameter tampering

out_of_scope: Stored XSS via image_url field (no HTML rendering of user content)
notes: Each injected vulnerability must map to exactly one CWE listed above.
Vulnerabilities must be injected on task branches, not on main.
No comments, TODO markers, or variable name hints near injected code.
Each exploit PoC must be a working Playwright script in evaluation/exploits/.
